ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (New Testament) Go to the [Bible Hub Complete Biblical Timeline](https://biblehub.com/timeline/) to find dates to put in your Book of Centuries.
[ ] (New Testament) Watch[ Luke's Dispatches](http://watch%20luke's%20dispatches%20which%20begin%20in%20the%20book%20of%20acts%20but%20tell%20about%20the%20spread%20of%20the%20gospel%20in%20our%20present%20day%20world.%20you%20can%20sign%20up%20for%20a%20free%20account/) which begin in the book of Acts but tell about the spread of the gospel in our present day world. You can sign up for a free account.
[ ] (New Testament) Explore the virtual resources at the [Museum of the Bible](https://www.museumofthebible.org/museum-from-home). (Note: a teacher should preview.)

[ ] (New Testament) Create a "[Resurrection Garden](https://seedskidsworship.com/resurrection-garden/)." (These can also be used as centerpieces for Easter dinner.)
[ ] (New Testament) Watch [Luke's Dispatches](https://frontlinemissions.info/store/p/lukes-dispatches-1-3) which begin in the book of Acts but tell about the spread of the gospel in our present day world. You can sign up for a free account.
[ ] (New Testament) This [website](https://www.opendoorsuk.org/persecution/world-watch-list/) lists the countries where Christians are still persecuted today and how we can pray for them.
[ ] (New Testament) Share a meal with others such as taking donations to a food bank, taking a meal to a neighbor, or baking cookies for a local fire or police station.
